  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The excerpt advances a specific contribution claim ('first learned cardinality estimator with calibrated uncertainty') and a gap statement about point predictors lacking calibration; assessing whether the thesis is coherent and the gap real is a plausible core task under any reading of 'check this'.",
    "R1.2": "'Check this' could mean verifying that the chain of reasoning—from motivation (multiplicative error propagation, silent drift) through design (conformal wrapper on autoregressive backbone) to conclusion (safe deployment prerequisite)—is internally consistent without logical gaps.",
    "R1.3": "Concrete numeric claims are present (median q-error 1.7 vs 1.8; 99th-pct drop from 89 to 14; 91% empirical coverage at 90% nominal); checking whether these figures actually support the stated conclusions is a plausible load-bearing interpretation.",
    "R1.4": "The paper asserts CalibCard is the 'first' of its kind; verifying that claim against the three cited prior lines of work (Naru, DeepDB, NeuroCard) and any uncited literature is a natural component of 'checking'.",
    "R1.6": "'Check this' could equally mean a prose and clarity review—sentence quality, precision of technical language, and paragraph-level coherence in the abstract and introduction."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I focus primarily on technical accuracy—verifying logical consistency of the reasoning and alignment of the numerical results with the stated claims—or on writing quality and prose clarity?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The excerpt advances a specific contribution claim ('first learned cardinality estimator with calibrated uncertainty') and a gap statement about point predictors lacking calibration; assessing whether the thesis is coherent and the gap real is a plausible core task under any reading of 'check this'.
rationale.R1.2: 'Check this' could mean verifying that the chain of reasoning—from motivation (multiplicative error propagation, silent drift) through design (conformal wrapper on autoregressive backbone) to conclusion (safe deployment prerequisite)—is internally consistent without logical gaps.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete numeric claims are present (median q-error 1.7 vs 1.8; 99th-pct drop from 89 to 14; 91% empirical coverage at 90% nominal); checking whether these figures actually support the stated conclusions is a plausible load-bearing interpretation.
rationale.R1.4: The paper asserts CalibCard is the 'first' of its kind; verifying that claim against the three cited prior lines of work (Naru, DeepDB, NeuroCard) and any uncited literature is a natural component of 'checking'.
rationale.R1.6: 'Check this' could equally mean a prose and clarity review—sentence quality, precision of technical language, and paragraph-level coherence in the abstract and introduction.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on technical accuracy—verifying logical consistency of the reasoning and alignment of the numerical results with the stated claims—or on writing quality and prose clarity?
